title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
description: A sharp, character-driven novel about first impressions, social expectations, and the slow work of mutual understanding.

title: Jane Eyre
author: Charlotte Bronte
description: An introspective coming-of-age story that balances moral conviction, emotional intensity, and gothic atmosphere.

title: Wuthering Heights
author: Emily Bronte
description: A stormy, layered novel of obsession, inheritance, and the destructive force of unresolved grief.

title: Moby-Dick
author: Herman Melville
description: A seafaring epic that turns a whale hunt into a study of ambition, symbolism, and obsession.

title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
description: A foundational detective collection featuring clever investigations, memorable villains, and precise deduction.

title: Dracula
author: Bram Stoker
description: An epistolary horror novel that builds dread through pursuit, folklore, and Victorian anxieties.

title: Frankenstein
author: Mary Shelley
description: A pioneering speculative novel about creation, responsibility, and the human cost of isolation.

title: The Count of Monte Cristo
author: Alexandre Dumas
description: A sweeping revenge story built on imprisonment, reinvention, justice, and patience.

title: Little Women
author: Louisa May Alcott
description: A warm, enduring portrait of four sisters shaping their values, ambitions, and independence.

title: The Secret Garden
author: Frances Hodgson Burnett
description: A restorative story of friendship, nature, and emotional renewal told through a hidden place brought back to life.

title: A Tale of Two Cities
author: Charles Dickens
description: A novel of sacrifice and social upheaval set against the violence and momentum of the French Revolution.

title: Great Expectations
author: Charles Dickens
description: A bildungsroman about class, ambition, shame, and the painful difference between self-image and reality.

title: The Picture of Dorian Gray
author: Oscar Wilde
description: A stylish, unsettling novel about vanity, moral decay, and the cost of preserving appearances.

title: The Time Machine
author: H. G. Wells
description: A compact speculative adventure that uses time travel to examine class, progress, and decline.

title: The War of the Worlds
author: H. G. Wells
description: An invasion narrative that combines scientific imagination with panic, survival, and imperial reversal.

title: Treasure Island
author: Robert Louis Stevenson
description: A classic pirate adventure packed with maps, shifting loyalties, and unforgettable storytelling momentum.

title: The Call of the Wild
author: Jack London
description: A survival tale that follows instinct, adaptation, and the pull of the untamed world.

title: The Scarlet Letter
author: Nathaniel Hawthorne
description: A morally layered novel about judgment, secrecy, and dignity under public scrutiny.

title: The Republic
author: Plato
description: A foundational philosophical dialogue exploring justice, education, governance, and the shape of an ideal society.

title: Meditations
author: Marcus Aurelius
description: A concise collection of Stoic reflections focused on discipline, perspective, and ethical self-governance.
